$comment: Config for the workflow plugin (~/.config/opencode/plugins/workflow.ts). A project can override any key via <worktree>/.opencode/workflow.json. Restart opencode after editing — the model list is baked into the tool description at startup. 'models' is the allowlist for per-agent model selection in workflow scripts; the 'note' fields are shown to the agent to guide selection. Empty list = agents always use the session default model.

models.note: Default choice. Use for implementation, code changes, browser-use fallback when Luna gets stuck, and all other general tasks. Use low, medium or high effort. Give explicit stop conditions.

Orchestrator and advisor. It is slow and expensive, so invoke selectively. Use for orchestrating or planning sub-workflows, and as an advisor/fallback when another model is stuck or has failed. Do not use for routine implementation or bulk stages. High effort only.

Code reviewer and judge. Use for reviewing Sol's implementations, devil's advocate, countering assumptions, judging panels. Ask for full coverage and filter the output downstream in the workflow. High effort only.

Data gatherer and browser verifier. It is fast but dumb, so invoke selectively. Use for scouting, file/symbol discovery, grep sweeps, classification, and browser use. Every browser use or verification task must include an instruction to stop and return if it gets stuck, and must have a Sol fallback agent setup to take over for it. Never use for producing code or content, review, judging, design. High effort only.
